column2: Description (This field is optional, you can leave it blank if you want to.)
column3: Document Content (Mandatory, set one of the following values: 0 for Use Content, 1 for Use Attachments, 2 for Use URL)
column4: Content Editor Text (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "Use Content".)
column5: URL (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "URL".)
column6: GRC Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column7: Policy Reviewer Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column8: Tags (Optional and accepts tags separated by "|". For example "Critical|SOX|PCI".)
column9: Published Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column10: Next Review Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column11: Policy Labels (Optional, set the value of the name of the label you want to use. Label names can be obtained from Asset Management / Asset Identification / Settings / Labels.)
column12: Related Projects (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a project, you can find them at Security Operations / Project Management.)
column13: Status (Mandatory, set value: 0 for Draft, 1 for Published)
column14: Document Type (You need to provide the name of the policy type, you can obtain the name of type from Control Catalogue / Policies / Settings / Document Type.)
column16: Policy Portal Permissions for this Document (Mandatory, set one of the following values: public for Public (Everyone can see the document), private for Private (Document is not shown on the portal), custom-roles for Only specific groups)
column17: Custom Roles (Mandatory in case you set permission to custom-roles, you can set more of following values: Owners for GRC Contact, Collaborators for Policy Reviewer Contact)
column18: Related Documents (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a policy, you can find them at Control Catalogue / Policies.)

column4:
ICS/OT Security Policy


Introduction


The ICS/OT Security Policy establishes the standards and practices for securing our Industrial Control Systems and Operational Technology. This policy aligns with the commitment to defend these systems against cyber threats, ensuring their uninterrupted operation, safety, and the prevention of cyberattacks that could jeopardize the organization's mission.


Objectives


  • Strengthen the security framework around Industrial Control Systems and Operational Technology.

  • Preserve the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of ICS/OT assets.

  • Align the operation of ICS/OT with the organization's broader cybersecurity strategy.

  • Ensure resilience and prompt response to cybersecurity incidents in the ICS/OT environment.

Scope


This policy pertains to all ICS/OT assets, including but not limited to controllers, sensors, actuators, network devices, software, and other associated equipment and technologies. All personnel, contractors, and authorized users engaged with these systems are bound by this policy.


Standards


General Rules:


  • All ICS/OT devices must be meticulously documented in the Asset Inventory, emphasizing their significance in production and operational processes.

  • The connection between ICS networks and other networks (e.g., corporate networks) must maintain strict physical and virtual segmentation.

  • External connections, such as the internet, wireless, or remote access, should also uphold strict segmentation.

  • External storage media usage within the ICS/OT environment must be stringently limited and undergo thorough security scans before use.

  • Mobile devices should not be connected to the ICS/OT network.

Network and System Requirements:


  • Continuous monitoring and activation of cybersecurity event logs on the ICS/OT networks and their connections are mandatory.

  • Systems must be routinely reviewed, configured securely, and hardened against vulnerabilities.

  • Implement strict controls around Safety Instrumental Systems (SIS) to maintain their isolation.

  • Effective vulnerability management for the ICS/OT infrastructure must be in place.

  • Implement a robust patch management process tailored for ICS/OT systems.

  • Employ dedicated cybersecurity applications for virus and malware protection within the ICS environment.

Data and Application Requirements:


  • Data exchanged with or generated by ICS/OT systems must be classified and handled according to its sensitivity and operational significance.

  • Applications and software utilized in the ICS/OT landscape should be sourced from trusted vendors and must be routinely assessed for vulnerabilities.

Employee and Contractor Guidelines:


  • Employees and contractors must undergo specialized training related to ICS/OT cybersecurity practices.

  • Any observed anomalies, malfunctions, or potential cyber threats must be promptly reported.

Monitoring and Review:


  • Regular audits will ensure that ICS/OT assets are compliant with this policy.

  • Any deviations identified will be addressed immediately and might necessitate additional training or system adjustments.

  • The dynamic nature of cybersecurity threats to ICS/OT systems necessitates frequent policy reviews and updates to ensure the organization remains resilient against emerging risks.
